provenance.summary: Heritabilities, additive genetic standard deviations and genetic correlations representative of literature consensus for North American beef cattle. Unlike the USMARC across-breed adjustment factors, beef-cattle genetic parameters have no single official table - published estimates vary by population, model and study. The values below are mid-range consensus figures suitable as a default; a researcher should override them with population-specific estimates where available.
primary_sources: Koots, K.R., Gibson, J.P., Smith, C., Wilton, J.W. (1994). Analyses of published genetic parameter estimates for beef production traits. 1. Heritability. Animal Breeding Abstracts 62:309-338.

Koots, K.R., Gibson, J.P., Wilton, J.W. (1994). Analyses of published genetic parameter estimates for beef production traits. 2. Phenotypic and genetic correlations. Animal Breeding Abstracts 62:825-853.
Beef Improvement Federation, Guidelines for Uniform Beef Improvement Programs (guidelines.beefimprovement.org).
American Angus Association, American Simmental Association / International Genetic Solutions, and American Hereford Association national cattle evaluation technical documentation.

Markel, C.D., Lake, S.L., Field, S.P., Holt, T.N., Engle, T.E., Gifford, C.L., Cunningham-Hollinger, H.C. A latent phenotype framework for pulmonary arterial pressure: boundary-aware transformation improves sire-associated heritability signal across independent Angus cattle datasets.
provenance.caveat: These are consensus defaults, not an authoritative single-source table. Every value is overridable; runs record which parameter-set version was used.
provenance.pap_note: PAP and PAP_L (latent-z) heritabilities are sire-model estimates from Markel et al.: raw mean PAP h2 = 0.250, boundary-aware latent-z PAP h2 = 0.318. The latent-z genetic SD is on the logit-transformed scale (practical PAP range 30-150 mmHg). PAP EPDs are published only by AAA, ARA (Red Angus) and ASA; override with population-specific estimates where available.
